ncert_truth_statements: The resistance of a conductor depends on its length, cross-sectional area, and the material it is made of: R = ρL/A
Resistivity (ρ) is a property of the material alone and does not depend on the shape or size of the conductor
For metallic conductors, resistivity increases with temperature because more frequent collisions impede electron drift
The SI unit of resistivity is ohm-metre (Ω·m); metals have very low resistivity while insulators have very high resistivity
A longer wire has greater resistance for the same material and cross-section; a thicker wire has lower resistance
